intro: Hieracium pseudosilsinum, also known as Silesian hawkweed, is a flowering plant species within the dandelion-like Hawkweed genus, characterized by its yellow or orange daisy-like flowers and bitter foliage. Traditionally, Silesian hawkweed has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium pseudosilsinum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as pseudosilin and silisin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Silesian hawkweed include tinctures, infusions, and dried extracts, often used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
